name: manifest
description: Install and configure the Manifest observability plugin for your agents. Use when setting up telemetry, configuring API keys, or troubleshooting the plugin.
capabilities: Install the Manifest plugin via Claude CLI
Configure API keys and custom endpoints for Manifest
Verify plugin installation by checking gateway logs
Troubleshoot common connection and configuration issues
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill uses Claude CLI commands to install and configure the Manifest observability plugin - this is the intended functionality. Network calls go to the official Manifest service (app.manifest.build). API key handling includes explicit warnings against logging secrets. Filesystem access is limited to reading standard gateway logs for verification.
valueStatement: This skill helps you install and configure the Manifest observability plugin for Claude Code agents, enabling telemetry and monitoring for your AI workflows.
limitations: Only works with the Manifest platform (app.manifest.build)
Requires user to provide their own API key
Does not provide built-in alerting or dashboard creation

useCases.targetUser: Developers running Claude agents in production
useCases.title: Enable agent observability
useCases.description: Set up telemetry for a Claude agent to monitor its behavior and performance over time

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise users with self-hosted observability infrastructure
useCases.title: Connect to self-hosted Manifest
useCases.description: Configure the plugin to connect to a company's internal Manifest instance instead of the cloud service

useCases.title: Debug plugin issues
useCases.description: Troubleshoot connection problems or configuration errors with the Manifest plugin

promptTemplates.title: Basic plugin setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @manifest to set up observability for my agent.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to enable monitoring for their Claude agent

promptTemplates.title: Setup with custom endpoint
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @manifest to connect my agent to my self-hosted Manifest instance at https://manifest.internal.company.com/api/v1/otlp
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to use a self-hosted Manifest instance

promptTemplates.title: Troubleshoot connection
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @manifest to check why my agent is not showing up in the Manifest dashboard
promptTemplates.scenario: User is having issues with the plugin connection

promptTemplates.title: Reconfigure API key
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @manifest to update my API key - the old one expired
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs to rotate their Manifest API key

bestPractices: Always stop the gateway before making configuration changes to avoid issues
Use the default endpoint unless you specifically need to self-host
Check gateway logs first when debugging any plugin issues
antiPatterns: Do not skip the gateway stop step - it can cause configuration to fail
Do not use API keys that do not start with 'mnfst_' - they are invalid
Do not share your API key in plain text - it gives others access to your observability data

faq.question: What is Manifest?
faq.answer: Manifest is an observability platform that helps you monitor and analyze your AI agent's behavior, performance, and usage patterns.

faq.question: Do I need an account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to create an account at app.manifest.build to get an API key for connecting your agent.

faq.question: Can I use a self-hosted version of Manifest?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure a custom endpoint URL if you self-host Manifest. The skill will prompt you for this during setup.

faq.question: What does the API key look like?
faq.answer: Manifest API keys always start with 'mnfst_'. If your key has a different format, it is invalid.

faq.question: How do I verify the plugin is working?
faq.answer: The skill checks the gateway logs for '[manifest] Observability pipeline active' to confirm successful setup.

faq.question: What if I get a 'connection refused' error?
faq.answer: Check that the endpoint URL is correct and reachable. If using the default endpoint, verify your network can access app.manifest.build.
